title: Fix Layout: mobile nav clipping + duplicate nav accessibility + replications link disabled
description:
There are THREE issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: Mobile nav items clipped on left edge
When the hamburger menu is opened on mobile (375px), nav items are clipped — 'Home' shows as 'me', 'Catalog' as 'log', etc. A previous fix added pl-2 to the mobile menu panel but it's not enough. The issue is likely that the mobile menu panel is positioned or sized incorrectly, or the nav links inside have negative margin/offset. Fix by:
- Ensuring the mobile menu panel has adequate left padding (at least pl-4 or px-4)
- Making sure no parent container has overflow:hidden that clips the text
- Check if the mobile menu div is positioned within the nav's padded container correctly

### Issue 2: Duplicate navigation links for screen readers
The desktop nav and mobile nav are both in the DOM simultaneously. Screen readers announce every link twice (35 anchors, 17 unique destinations). Fix by:
- Adding aria-hidden='true' to the mobile menu when it's hidden, and removing it when shown
- Adding aria-hidden='true' to the desktop nav at mobile breakpoints (or use role='presentation' on the hidden one)
- The mobile menu toggle JS should update aria-hidden when toggling

### Issue 3: Replications link in 'More' dropdown appears disabled
The Replications link in the 'More' dropdown menu appears greyed out and doesn't respond to clicks, even though /replications is a working page. Check if there's a CSS rule or class that's disabling this specific link. It might be styled with pointer-events:none, opacity, or a disabled class. Find and remove whatever is preventing the link from being clickable.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Fix timeline era tabs: truncated with no scroll indicator
description:
On the Timeline page, the horizontal era navigation tabs extend beyond the viewport — 'Era 4' is cut off as 'Era 4: T...' with no scroll indicator or overflow handling. Users may not realize there are more eras to explore.

Fix by:
1. Make the era tab container horizontally scrollable (overflow-x: auto)
2. Add scroll-snap for nice tab snapping
3. Optionally add a subtle gradient fade on the right edge to indicate more content
4. Ensure the tabs don't get truncated — use whitespace-nowrap on each tab
5. On mobile, the tabs should be swipeable/scrollable

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Improve sparse glossary: add more terms or remove empty letter indicators
description:
The Glossary only defines 16 terms, leaving most alphabet letters greyed out and empty. This makes the glossary feel incomplete for a research library.

The best fix: remove the alphabetical letter index/navigation that prominently displays empty letters. Instead, just show all terms in a simple alphabetical list or grouped by letter (only showing letters that have terms). This way, the glossary doesn't advertise its gaps.

If there's an alphabet bar/index at the top showing A-Z with greyed out empty letters, either:
- Remove it entirely, OR
- Only show letters that have at least one term

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.
